title: Intense Korean Cabbage Stew
description: A hearty, one-pot Korean dish featuring fermented and chewy hispi sweetheart cabbage, this stew is perfect for a cold winter's night. It is also onion and garlic free, making it suitable for those with sensitivities.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced zucchini and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the cubed tofu and cook until it is lightly browned.
Add the fermented cabbage, gochujang, soy sauce, korean chili flakes, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Stir everything together, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Use two forks to knead the cabbage and break it down into smaller pieces.
Continue to simmer the stew for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cabbage is very tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped green onions if desired.
